Second I/O Collection Period (15 mg and 30 mg)
SRC Safety Data Review Through Day 23
Expected Steady State (based on 5x half-life of 2 days)
Predose PK/PD Sampling Days (15 mg accumulation)
Predose PK/PD Sampling Days (15 mg steady state and 30 mg)
Zinc Washout Period Prior to Day 1

Fasting Before Serum Chemistry at Screening and Check-in
ALT Elevation > 3x ULN
data.description: ALT greater than 3 times upper limit of normal triggers dose modification
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN requires dose reduction or temporary interruption depending on accompanying conditions

data.name: ALT Elevation > 5x ULN
data.description: ALT greater than 5 times upper limit of normal triggers temporary interruption
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing

data.name: Hemoglobin Decrease > 20% from Baseline
data.description: Hemoglobin decrease greater than 20% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: Hemoglobin > 20% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD

data.name: Platelet Count Decrease > 25% from Baseline
data.description: Platelet count decrease greater than 25% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: Platelet count > 25% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD

data.name: Neutrophil Count Decrease > 30% from Baseline
data.description: Neutrophil count decrease greater than 30% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: Neutrophil count > 30% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.description: Bilirubin elevation accompanied by ALT elevation indicative of liver injury
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN indicative of liver injury requires temporary interruption

data.name: Neurological Worsening
data.description: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or neurologic physical exam assessment
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or by neurologic physical exam assessment requires evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor for dose modification

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening
data.description: Clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening including suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening which may include but not limited to suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis requires evaluation for dose modification

data.name: SRC Safety Review Clearance for Dose Titration
data.description: Safety Review Committee must review available safety data through Day 23 before titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Before titration to 30 mg/day, the Safety Review Committee (SRC) will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant

data.name: Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.description: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for study intervention discontinuation
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction

data.name: Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.description: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for study intervention discontinuation
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection

data.name: Use of Disallowed Medication
data.description: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for study intervention discontinuation
data.text: Use of disallowed medication

data.name: Pregnancy or Planned Pregnancy
data.description: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for study intervention discontinuation
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy

data.name: Clinically Significant QTcF Change
data.description: Clinically significant change from baseline in QTcF after enrollment
data.text: If a clinically significant finding is identified including but not limited to changes from baseline in QTcF after enrollment, the Investigator will determine if the participant can continue

data.name: Maximum Rechallenge Limit
data.description: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed for dose modifications
data.text: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed

data.name: Safety Concern Preventing Dose Titration
data.description: Safety concerns that may place participants at undue risk prevent titration to 30 mg
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study

data.name: Screening to Enrollment/Run-in Transition
data.text: Participants eligible for the study will be enrolled on Day -7 after meeting all inclusion/exclusion criteria and signing ICF. Participants are registered and assigned a participant number.

data.name: Run-in to Treatment Period 1 Transition
data.text: Following the Run-in Period (Day -7 through Day -1), participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day starting Day 1. Chelator therapy must be discontinued approximately 4 days prior and zinc therapy at least 21 days prior to Day 1.

data.name: Inpatient Period 1 to Outpatient Period Transition
data.text: Participants may be discharged from the CRU on Day 9 to return on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose). At the CRU's discretion, participants may remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period to Inpatient Period 2 Transition
data.text: Participants return to CRU on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose); all procedures will start on Day 23. Outpatient Period may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval; participant will continue Inpatient Period 2 at Day 23.

data.name: Treatment Period 1 to Treatment Period 2 Dose Titration
data.text: ALXN1840 dose increased from 15 mg/day to 30 mg/day on Day 29. Before titration, the SRC will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant. If safety concerns exist, participants may remain on 15 mg/day for the duration of the study.

data.name: Treatment Period 2 to Follow-up/EOS Transition
data.text: Following completion of both inpatient periods (Day 40), participants transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or consider continuation of ALXN1840. All participants should return for EOS Visit on Day 54 +/- 2 days.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN requires dose reduction to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality. Weekly repeat testing required.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for ALT > 5x ULN
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when ALT is below 3 × ULN.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hemoglobin Decrease > 20%
data.text: Hemoglobin > 20% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Platelet Count Decrease > 25%
data.text: Platelet count > 25% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Neutrophil Count Decrease > 30%
data.text: Neutrophil count > 30% decrease from baseline requires dose reduction to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN indicative of liver injury requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when bilirubin is below ULN, requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Neurological Worsening Dose Evaluation
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or neurologic physical exam assessment: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale must be documented and re-evaluated at next study visit.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening Dose Evaluation
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale must be documented and re-evaluated at next study visit.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Use of Disallowed Medication
data.text: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Abnormal Liver Function
data.text: Discontinuation of study intervention for abnormal liver function should be considered by the Investigator when a participant meets one of the conditions outlined in the Dose Modification criteria (Section 6.6) or if the Investigator believes it is in the best interest of the participant.

data.name: Discontinuation by Investigator or Alexion Determination
data.text: Alexion or the Investigator deems it is necessary for the participant. Investigators must notify Alexion immediately of study intervention discontinuation. The decision to discontinue should not be delayed for causality assessment.

data.name: Participant Voluntary Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request. The participant will be permanently discontinued both from the study intervention and from the study at that time.

data.name: Investigator-Initiated Withdrawal
data.description: Participant may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioral, compliance, or administrative reasons.

data.name: Study Intervention Discontinuation with Safety Follow-up
data.description: If study intervention is definitively discontinued, the participant should remain in the study to be evaluated for safety follow-up. An early discontinuation visit should be conducted as shown in the SoA.

data.name: Lost to Follow-up
data.description: A participant will be considered lost to follow-up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site. At least 3 telephone calls and a certified letter must be attempted before deeming lost to follow-up.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: A participant is considered to have completed the study if he/she has completed all phases of the study including the last scheduled procedure shown in the SoA. End of Study Visit on Day 54 +/- 2 days.

data.name: Study Termination by Alexion
data.description: Alexion reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of Alexion. Reasons may include failure to comply with protocol, inadequate recruitment, or discontinuation of further study intervention development.

data.name: SRC Safety Review for Dose Titration Decision
data.description: Before titration to 30 mg/day on Day 29, the Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant. If safety concerns exist, participants may remain on 15 mg/day for the duration of the study.

data.name: ALT-Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose reduction (ALT > 3x ULN) or temporary interruption (ALT > 5x ULN) based on liver function test results. Weekly repeat testing required.

data.name: Hematology-Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose reduction based on hemoglobin (>20% decrease), platelet count (>25% decrease), or neutrophil count (>30% decrease) from baseline.

data.name: Liver Injury Assessment Decision (Bilirubin + ALT)
data.description: Decision point for temporary interruption when bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN indicative of liver injury. Rechallenge requires Alexion Medical Monitor approval.

data.name: Neurological Worsening Assessment Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose modification evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data when neurologic worsening is observed.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening Assessment Decision
data.description: Decision point for dose modification evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data when clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening is observed.

data.name: Rechallenge Limit Decision
data.description: Decision point to determine if rechallenge is permissible. A maximum of 3 rechallenges are allowed. Participants on 15 mg QOD should be rechallenged at 15 mg QOD dose.

data.name: CRU Discharge vs Continued Inpatient Decision (Day 9)
data.description: At the CRU's discretion, participants may be discharged on Day 9 or remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period Extension Decision
data.description: The Outpatient Period during Treatment Period 1 may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval. Additional investigational product will be provided for daily dosing.

data.name: Post-Study Therapy Decision
data.description: Following completion of both inpatient periods (Day 40), participants will either transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or at the discretion of the treating physician consider continuation of ALXN1840 under preapproval access.

data.name: QTcF Clinical Significance Decision
data.description: If a clinically significant finding in QTcF is identified after enrollment, the Investigator determines if the participant can continue in the study and if any change in participant management is needed.
